_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-14R Aspirin (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-14R: Aspirin — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) — suspected STEMI or NSTEMI
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to aspirin or NSAIDs
Active GI bleeding or bleeding disorder (relative contraindication — assess with online MC)
precautions: Ask whether patient has already taken aspirin since pain onset — supplement only if insufficient dose taken (total target 324-325 mg)
Chew and swallow — do not swallow whole; chewing improves absorption speed
ACS indication only in Michigan EMS protocol — do not administer for non-ACS chest pain
expected_effects: Antiplatelet effect — inhibits thromboxane A2 to reduce further platelet aggregation at coronary thrombus
Onset of antiplatelet effect within minutes when chewed
notes: Target dose: up to 324-325 mg chewed and swallowed
If patient already took aspirin since pain onset: assess dose taken; supplement to reach ~325 mg total
ACS indication ONLY — do not use for fever, pain, or other indications in EMS protocol
No pediatric dosing in Michigan EMS protocol for this indication — ACS is adult-predominant presentation

dosing.dose: Up to 325 mg chewed and swallowed
dosing.notes: Supplement if patient already took aspirin since pain onset; total target ~324-325 mg; chew do not swallow whole
